mathml_representations_list:
<mstyle id="custom-group-0" class="custom-group"><mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_85">σ</mi>
          <mrow>
            <mo>(</mo>
            <mrow>
              <mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_86">u</mi>
            </mrow>
            <mo>)</mo>
          </mrow>
          </mstyle>

<mstyle id="custom-group-1" class="custom-group"><mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_87">ε</mi>
          <mrow>
            <mo>(</mo>
            <mrow>
              <mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_88">v</mi>
            </mrow>
            <mo>)</mo>
          </mrow>
          </mstyle>

concepts_list.name: u_field
concepts_list.description: Primal unknown field (temperature or displacement) in the domain
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'u' appears multiple times as a bold-italic variable representing the primary unknown in both thermal and elastostatic problems, with its value on boundaries denoted by overbar. It is used in weak forms and is the solution sought in the abstract formulation.

concepts_list.name: v_test
concepts_list.description: Test function in the variational formulation, used in the weak form
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'v' appears repeatedly as a bold-italic test function in the weak forms of both thermal and elastostatic problems, with the condition 'for all v in V' indicating its role as a test function.

concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.description: Main computational domain in which the problem is defined
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Ω' is used to denote the domain of interest, defined as a subset of R^d, and appears in integrals over the domain, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Γ_t.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_t_boundary
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary where prescribed traction or heat flux is applied
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Γ_t' is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction or heat flux) are prescribed, and appears in the weak form as the integration domain for the boundary term.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_interface
concepts_list.description: Internal interfaces between subdomains in a multi-phase domain
concepts_list.justification: The paper defines internal interfaces between subdomains as Γ_ij, and refers to them as part of the boundary structure, indicating their role as internal boundaries between distinct phases.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Volume differential element in the domain integral
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΩ' appears in integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω, and is standardly used to denote the volume element in integration.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Surface differential element in the boundary integral
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΓ' appears in integrals over boundaries like Γ_t and internal interfaces, and is used to denote the surface element in boundary integrals.

concepts_list.name: sigma_u
concepts_list.description: Cauchy stress tensor defined as C times strain tensor
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'σ(u)' is explicitly defined as the Cauchy stress tensor, with σ = Cε, and is used in the elastostatic weak form as a bilinear form involving the strain tensor.

concepts_list.name: epsilon_v
concepts_list.description: Linearized strain tensor evaluated at the test function v
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'ε(v)' is defined as (1/2)(∇v + ∇v^T), and is used in the elastostatic weak form as the strain tensor for the test function v.

concepts_list.name: b_body_force
concepts_list.description: Body force vector (e.g., gravity) applied throughout the domain
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'b' appears as a bold-italic vector in the elastostatic weak form and is described as the body force, applied over the entire domain.

concepts_list.name: t_bar
concepts_list.description: Prescribed traction vector on the Neumann boundary
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 't' with overbar is used to denote the prescribed traction on the boundary Γ_t, and appears in the weak form as a boundary condition.

concepts_list.name: V_function_space
concepts_list.description: Test function space in the weak formulation
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'V' is used as the space of test functions, and appears in the condition 'for all v in V', indicating it is a function space where test functions are defined.

concepts_list.name: U_function_space
concepts_list.description: Solution function space in the weak formulation
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'U' is used to denote the space of admissible solutions, where the solution u is required to satisfy Dirichlet boundary conditions on Γ_u.

new_concept_info.description:
Primal unknown field (temperature or displacement) in the domain
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'u' appears multiple times as a bold-italic variable representing the primary unknown in both thermal and elastostatic problems, with its value on boundaries denoted by overbar. It is used in weak forms and is the solution sought in the abstract formulation.

Test function in the variational formulation, used in the weak form
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'v' appears repeatedly as a bold-italic test function in the weak forms of both thermal and elastostatic problems, with the condition 'for all v in V' indicating its role as a test function.

Main computational domain in which the problem is defined
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'Ω' is used to denote the domain of interest, defined as a subset of R^d, and appears in integrals over the domain, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Γ_t.

Neumann boundary where prescribed traction or heat flux is applied
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'Γ_t' is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction or heat flux) are prescribed, and appears in the weak form as the integration domain for the boundary term.

Volume differential element in the domain integral
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'dΩ' appears in integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω, and is standardly used to denote the volume element in integration.

Surface differential element in the boundary integral
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'dΓ' appears in integrals over boundaries like Γ_t and internal interfaces, and is used to denote the surface element in boundary integrals.

Cauchy stress tensor defined as C times strain tensor
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'σ(u)' is explicitly defined as the Cauchy stress tensor, with σ = Cε, and is used in the elastostatic weak form as a bilinear form involving the strain tensor.

Linearized strain tensor evaluated at the test function v
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'ε(v)' is defined as (1/2)(∇v + ∇v^T), and is used in the elastostatic weak form as the strain tensor for the test function v.

Body force vector (e.g., gravity) applied throughout the domain
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'b' appears as a bold-italic vector in the elastostatic weak form and is described as the body force, applied over the entire domain.

Prescribed traction vector on the Neumann boundary
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 't' with overbar is used to denote the prescribed traction on the boundary Γ_t, and appears in the weak form as a boundary condition.

Test function space in the weak formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol 'V' is used as the space of test functions, and appears in the condition 'for all v in V', indicating it is a function space where test functions are defined.
